seoTitle: Rohm Products | SiC MOSFETs, SBDs, IGBTs Distributor | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse Rohm SiC MOSFETs, Schottky barrier diodes, IGBTs, and power modules. Technical support and Selection guidance for automotive and industrial.

faqs.question: What product categories does Rohm offer?
faqs.answer: Rohm offers four main product categories: SiC MOSFETs featuring industry-leading trench-gate technology with ultra-low on-resistance; SiC Schottky Barrier Diodes with zero reverse recovery for high-efficiency rectification; IGBTs for high-power industrial applications; and Power Modules integrating multiple SiC devices for complete power solutions. Each category provides comprehensive voltage and current ratings to meet diverse application needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for Selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Rohm SiC MOSFET for my application?
faqs.answer: Rohm SiC MOSFET selection criteria: (1) Voltage rating - Choose 650V for 400V systems, 750V for OBC, 1200V for 800V systems, 1700V for high-voltage industrial. (2) Current rating - Select based on RMS current with 50% margin. (3) Rds(on) - Lower for higher efficiency, balance with cost. (4) Package - TO-247 for discrete, modules for high power. (5) Automotive - Select AEC-Q101 qualified for EV. (6) Gate drive - Rohm devices work with standard drivers. (7) Thermal - Consider cooling requirements. Contact BeiLuo FAE for detailed Selection support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Rohm's Selection guide or contact BeiLuo FAE for personalized selection assistance.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of Rohm SiC devices?
faqs.answer: Rohm SiC device advantages: (1) Ultra-low Rds(on) - Industry-leading specific on-resistance reduces conduction losses. (2) Trench-gate technology - Proprietary design optimized for performance. (3) Easy gate drive - Compatible with standard 15V/18V drivers. (4) Automotive qualified - Full AEC-Q101 with mass production track record. (5) High reliability - Japanese manufacturing quality. (6) Comprehensive portfolio - MOSFETs, SBDs, modules. (7) Application support - Extensive reference designs and FAE support. Rohm delivers best-in-class performance with ease of use.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Rohm for industry-leading low Rds(on) and proven automotive reliability.

categories.name: SiC MOSFETs
categories.description: Industry-leading trench-gate SiC MOSFETs with ultra-low on-resistance for automotive and industrial.
categories.longDescription: Rohm SiC MOSFETs utilize proprietary trench-gate technology to deliver industry-leading low on-resistance and excellent switching performance. Available in 650V, 750V, 1200V, and 1700V ratings, these devices serve applications from EV traction inverters to industrial motor drives. The easy gate drive compatibility and AEC-Q101 qualification make Rohm SiC MOSFETs ideal for demanding automotive applications. Contact BeiLuo for Selection guidance and technical support. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

products.shortDescription: 650V 100A trench-gate SiC MOSFET featuring ultra-low 22mΩ Rds(on) in TO-247-4L Kelvin source package for high-efficie...
descriptionParagraphs: The SCT3022AL is a 650V 100A Silicon Carbide MOSFET featuring Rohm's proprietary trench-gate technology with ultra-low 22mΩ on-resistance. This device is designed for high-power applications including EV traction inverters, solar inverters, and industrial motor drives where efficiency and thermal performance are critical.

The trench-gate structure delivers industry-leading low Rds(on) while maintaining excellent switching characteristics. The TO-247-4L package with Kelvin source connection enables fast switching and reduced switching losses. The device is fully AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications.

With its ultra-low on-resistance and easy gate drive compatibility, the SCT3022AL enables system designers to achieve higher efficiency and power density compared to traditional silicon IGBTs and MOSFETs.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The SCT3022AL is one of Rohm's flagship SiC MOSFETs with exceptional performance. The 22mΩ Rds(on) is among the lowest in the industry for 650V devices. For EV traction inverters, this device can deliver 98%+ efficiency. The Kelvin source connection is essential for maximizing switching performance. Highly recommended for high-power automotive and industrial applications.
faeReview.highlight: Industry-leading 22mΩ Rds(on) with Kelvin source for maximum performance

alternativeParts.reason: Lower conduction losses for high-current applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 150A+ applications requiring maximum efficiency
Use for 60-80A applications where cost is priority

companionParts.description: Isolated gate driver for SiC MOSFETs

faqs.question: What makes SCT3022AL's Rds(on) so low?
faqs.answer: SCT3022AL low Rds(on) factors: (1) Trench-gate technology - Proprietary design optimizes channel density. (2) Advanced process - State-of-the-art SiC manufacturing. (3) Cell design - Optimized unit cell structure. (4) Material quality - High-quality SiC substrates. (5) 22mΩ at 25°C - Industry-leading for 650V/100A. (6) Temperature stability - Low increase at high Tj. (7) Competitive advantage - Best-in-class performance. Rohm's trench-gate technology enables this exceptional performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SCT3022AL for applications requiring lowest conduction losses.

faqs.question: How does the Kelvin source connection improve performance?
faqs.answer: Kelvin source benefits: (1) Separate paths - Power and gate return separated. (2) No common inductance - Eliminates source inductance from gate loop. (3) Faster switching - Reduced turn-on/turn-off times. (4) Lower losses - Reduced switching energy. (5) Cleaner waveforms - Less ringing and overshoot. (6) Higher frequency - Can switch at 100kHz+. (7) Better control - Precise gate voltage control. The TO-247-4L package with Kelvin source is essential for high-speed SiC switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use Kelvin source connection for high-speed switching applications.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is optimal for SCT3022AL?
faqs.answer: SCT3022AL gate drive requirements: (1) Recommended Vgs - +18V for turn-on, 0V for turn-off. (2) Compatible range - +15V to +18V turn-on. (3) Threshold voltage - 2.8V typical. (4) Gate charge - 75nC total at 18V. (5) Gate resistor - 2-10Ω depending on speed. (6) Drive power - 3.5W at 50kHz. (7) Miller plateau - At 7V during switching. Rohm devices are designed for easy drive with standard 15-18V drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V gate drive with 5Ω gate resistor for optimal performance.

faqs.question: Can SCT3022AL be used for EV traction inverters?
faqs.answer: Yes, SCT3022AL is ideal for EV traction: (1) Voltage - 650V for 400V battery systems. (2) Current - 100A for 80-120kW inverters. (3) Efficiency - 98%+ achievable. (4) Automotive - AEC-Q101 qualified. (5) Reliability - Mass production proven. (6) Thermal - Excellent thermal performance. (7) Support - Full EV reference designs. Perfect for EV traction inverter applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 400V EV traction inverters up to 120kW.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for SCT3022AL?
faqs.answer: SCT3022AL thermal design: (1) Rth(j-c) - 0.35°C/W typical. (2) At 50A: Pcond = 55W. (3) Switching losses - 25W at 50kHz. (4) Total - ~80W at full load. (5) Required heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 1.0°C/W. (6) Cooling - Forced air or liquid. (7) Interface - High-performance thermal grease. Proper thermal design is essential for reliable operation at high power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air or liquid cooling; design for Tj < 150°C for automotive reliability.

faqs.question: How does SCT3022AL compare to silicon IGBTs?
faqs.answer: SCT3022AL vs IGBT comparison: (1) Rds(on) - 22mΩ vs 40mΩ equivalent. (2) Switching - 10x faster than IGBT. (3) Losses - 70% lower switching losses. (4) Temperature - 175°C vs 150°C. (5) Body diode - Usable vs none in IGBT. (6) Efficiency - 2-3% system improvement. (7) Size - Smaller magnetics. SCT3022AL significantly outperforms silicon IGBTs in all aspects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Replace IGBTs with SCT3022AL for higher efficiency and power density.

products.shortDescription: 1200V 80A trench-gate SiC MOSFET featuring 80mΩ Rds(on) in TO-247-4L package, optimized for 800V EV traction inverter...
descriptionParagraphs: The SCT3080KL is a 1200V 80A Silicon Carbide MOSFET featuring 80mΩ on-resistance. This device is optimized for 800V EV applications including traction inverters, onboard chargers, and DC-DC converters, as well as high-voltage solar inverters.

The trench-gate technology delivers excellent switching performance with easy gate drive compatibility. The device is fully AEC-Q101 qualified and has been proven in mass production automotive applications.
With its 1200V rating and automotive qualification, the SCT3080KL is the ideal choice for next-generation 800V EV platforms requiring high efficiency and reliability.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The SCT3080KL is Rohm's flagship 1200V device for 800V EV applications. The 80mΩ Rds(on) provides excellent efficiency for traction inverters and onboard chargers. Rohm has extensive mass production experience with this device in major OEM platforms. The easy gate drive compatibility simplifies design. Highly recommended for 800V EV powertrain applications.
faeReview.highlight: Proven in mass production 800V EV platforms with easy gate drive

alternativeParts.reason: Lower conduction losses for high-current traction

faqs.question: What applications is SCT3080KL best suited for?
faqs.answer: SCT3080KL optimal applications: (1) 800V EV traction - Perfect for 800V battery systems. (2) Onboard chargers - 11kW and 22kW OBC. (3) DC-DC converters - HV to LV conversion. (4) Solar inverters - 1000V DC bus systems. (5) Energy storage - Battery inverters. (6) Industrial drives - High-voltage motor drives. (7) Power supplies - High-voltage SMPS. The 1200V rating covers all 800V applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 800V EV traction, OBC, and high-voltage solar inverters.

faqs.question: How does SCT3080KL compare to 650V devices for 400V systems?
faqs.answer: 1200V vs 650V comparison: (1) Voltage margin - Higher safety margin for 400V. (2) Switching - Slightly slower due to higher voltage. (3) Rds(on) - Higher for same die size. (4) Cost - Higher device cost. (5) Applications - 1200V for 800V systems, 650V for 400V. (6) Efficiency - Comparable when properly designed. (7) Recommendation - Use 650V for 400V, 1200V for 800V. Select based on system voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SCT3080KL for 800V systems; 650V devices for 400V systems.

faqs.question: What is the mass production status of SCT3080KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3080KL production status: (1) Mass production - Full volume production. (2) Automotive - Multiple OEM platforms. (3) Volume - Millions of units shipped. (4) Quality - Zero defect track record. (5) Lead time - Standard 6-8 weeks. (6) Supply - Stable from Japan fab. (7) Support - Full PPAP available. Proven and reliable supply.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully qualified for automotive mass production with stable supply.

faqs.question: Can SCT3080KL be paralleled for higher power?
faqs.answer: SCT3080KL parallel operation: (1) Positive tempco - Good current sharing. (2) Gate drive - Common gate resistor. (3) Symmetry - Equal trace lengths critical. (4) Current - Can parallel 2-4 devices. (5) Applications - 300kW+ traction inverters. (6) Considerations - Thermal coupling. (7) Support - FAE can provide guidance. Parallel for ultra-high power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can parallel 2-4 devices for 300kW+ traction inverters.

faqs.question: What gate driver is recommended for SCT3080KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3080KL gate driver recommendations: (1) Voltage - +18V turn-on, 0V turn-off. (2) Isolation - 2500Vrms minimum. (3) Current - 5A peak minimum. (4) Features - Desaturation protection recommended. (5) Options - Rohm BM6104FV, TI UCC21732. (6) PCB - Minimize gate loop inductance. (7) Protection - Miller clamp recommended. Standard SiC gate drivers work well.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use isolated 18V gate driver with 5A+ peak current and desaturation protection.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of SCT3080KL in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: SCT3080KL automotive reliability: (1) MTBF - >1,000,000 hours. (2) Power cycling - 100,000+ cycles. (3) Temperature - 175°C maximum. (4) Automotive - AEC-Q101 Grade 0. (5) Field data - Proven in EVs. (6) Screening - 100% screening. (7) Warranty - Contact Rohm for terms. Excellent reliability for automotive.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for Tj < 150°C for 15+ year automotive lifetime.

The trench-gate technology delivers industry-leading low Rds(on) with excellent switching performance. The TO-247-4L package with Kelvin source connection enables fast switching and reduced switching losses. The device is fully AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications.

faqs.question: What makes SCT3040KL ideal for 800V EV applications?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL advantages for 800V EV: (1) Lowest Rds(on) - 40mΩ minimizes conduction losses. (2) 1200V rating - Safe for 800V battery systems. (3) High efficiency - 98%+ achievable in traction inverters. (4) Automotive qualified - AEC-Q101 Grade 0. (5) Proven reliability - Mass production track record. (6) Easy gate drive - Compatible with standard drivers. (7) Thermal performance - Low losses reduce cooling needs. Best choice for premium EV powertrain applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SCT3040KL for maximum efficiency in 800V EV traction and OBC applications.

faqs.question: How does SCT3040KL compare to SCT3080KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL vs SCT3080KL comparison: (1) Rds(on) - 40mΩ vs 80mΩ (50% lower). (2) Current - Optimized for 40A vs 80A. (3) Efficiency - Higher due to lower Rds(on). (4) Cost - Higher due to larger die. (5) Applications - SCT3040KL for high-efficiency traction, SCT3080KL for OBC/DC-DC. (6) Thermal - Lower losses with SCT3040KL. (7) Selection - Use SCT3040KL when efficiency is critical, SCT3080KL for cost-sensitive designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SCT3040KL for traction inverters; SCT3080KL for OBC and DC-DC converters.

faqs.question: What gate drive is recommended for SCT3040KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL gate drive requirements: (1) Voltage - +18V turn-on, 0V turn-off. (2) Current - 5A peak minimum for fast switching. (3) Isolation - 2500Vrms for 800V systems. (4) Desaturation - Recommended for protection. (5) Miller clamp - Helps prevent false turn-on. (6) Options - Rohm BM6104FV, TI UCC21732. (7) PCB - Minimize gate loop inductance. Standard 1200V SiC gate drivers work well with this device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use isolated 18V gate driver with desaturation protection and Miller clamp.

faqs.question: Can SCT3040KL be used for paralleled operation?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL parallel operation: (1) Positive tempco - Good current sharing characteristics. (2) Symmetry - Equal trace lengths essential. (3) Gate drive - Common gate resistor for each device. (4) Current sharing - 2-4 devices can be paralleled. (5) Applications - 300kW+ traction inverters. (6) Thermal - Consider thermal coupling between devices. (7) Support - FAE guidance available. Parallel operation enables ultra-high power applications with excellent current sharing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can parallel 2-4 devices for ultra-high power traction inverters >300kW.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for SCT3040KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL thermal design: (1) Rth(j-c) - 0.45°C/W typical. (2) At 30A: Pcond = 36W. (3) Switching losses - 15W at 50kHz. (4) Total - ~50W at full load. (5) Heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 1.5°C/W with forced air. (6) Interface - High-performance thermal grease. (7) Target - Tj < 150°C for automotive reliability. Lower losses than higher Rds(on) devices reduce thermal management requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling; design for Tj < 150°C for automotive lifetime.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency improvement with SCT3040KL?
faqs.answer: SCT3040KL efficiency benefits: (1) Traction inverter - 98.5% vs 97% with IGBT. (2) OBC - 97% vs 95% with silicon. (3) DC-DC - 99% vs 97% with silicon. (4) Loss reduction - 50% lower conduction losses vs 80mΩ devices. (5) System impact - Smaller heatsink, lighter thermal management. (6) Range impact - 3-5% improvement in EV range. (7) Cost benefit - Lower cooling costs offset device cost. The efficiency gains translate to real system benefits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 1-2% system efficiency improvement vs higher Rds(on) alternatives.

faqs.question: How much efficiency improvement does SCT3017AL provide?
faqs.answer: SCT3017AL efficiency benefits: (1) Vs 22mΩ - 0.5% higher efficiency at 100A. (2) Vs IGBT - 2-3% higher efficiency. (3) Traction inverter - 98.5%+ achievable. (4) Conduction losses - 23% lower than 22mΩ device. (5) System impact - 500W less heat in 100kW system. (6) Cooling - Smaller heatsink possible. (7) Payback - Efficiency gains offset cost in high-utilization applications. The efficiency improvement is significant in high-current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total cost of ownership including cooling savings to justify the premium.

faqs.question: What is the current rating of SCT3017AL?
faqs.answer: SCT3017AL current capabilities: (1) Continuous - 118A at 25°C case. (2) At 100°C case - ~80A continuous. (3) Pulse current - 300A for 1ms. (4) Practical limit - 100-120A for continuous operation. (5) Thermal limited - Depends on heatsink and cooling. (6) Safe operating area - Defined in datasheet. (7) Derating - Apply appropriate margin for reliability. The 118A rating makes this suitable for very high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 80-100A continuous with proper thermal management.

faqs.question: What makes SCT3017AL's Rds(on) so low?
faqs.answer: SCT3017AL low Rds(on) technology: (1) Advanced trench-gate - Optimized cell structure. (2) Larger die size - More silicon area for current flow. (3) Advanced process - State-of-the-art SiC manufacturing. (4) Material quality - High-quality SiC substrates. (5) Cell density - High channel density design. (6) 17mΩ at 25°C - Best-in-class for 650V. (7) Temperature stability - Low increase at high Tj. Rohm's continuous innovation enables this industry-leading performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Rohm's advanced trench-gate technology and larger die enable the 17mΩ performance.

faqs.question: Is SCT3017AL suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: SCT3017AL automotive qualifications: (1) AEC-Q101 - Full automotive qualification. (2) Grade 0 - -40°C to +175°C. (3) PPAP available - For production approval. (4) Traceability - Full lot traceability. (5) Reliability - Proven in field applications. (6) Screening - 100% screening. (7) Quality - Japanese manufacturing standards. Fully qualified for the most demanding automotive applications including EV traction inverters.

products.shortDescription: 650V 20A SiC Schottky Barrier Diode with zero reverse recovery, ideal for PFC and output rectification applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SCH2080KE is a 650V 20A Silicon Carbide Schottky Barrier Diode featuring zero reverse recovery charge and fast switching characteristics. This device is designed for high-efficiency power conversion applications including PFC circuits, output rectifiers, and freewheeling diodes.

The SiC Schottky technology eliminates reverse recovery losses, significantly improving efficiency in high-frequency switching applications. The device features low forward voltage drop and excellent thermal performance, enabling compact and efficient power supply designs.

With its 650V rating and 20A current capability, the SCH2080KE is ideal for EV onboard chargers, solar inverters, industrial power supplies, and motor drive applications where efficiency and reliability are critical.
features: Fast switching with no reverse recovery

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The SCH2080KE is my go-to SiC SBD for 650V applications. The zero reverse recovery is a game-changer for PFC circuits - you eliminate the switching losses associated with silicon diodes. At 20A, it handles most PFC and rectifier applications with ease. The forward drop of 1.35V is competitive, and the thermal performance is excellent. I've used this in numerous EV OBC and solar inverter designs with great results. Pair it with Rohm SiC MOSFETs for the ultimate high-efficiency solution.
faeReview.highlight: Zero reverse recovery enables highest efficiency PFC designs

alternativeParts.useCase: Use when TO-220AC package is preferred

faqs.question: What is reverse recovery and why does SiC SBD eliminate it?
faqs.answer: Reverse recovery explained: (1) Silicon diodes - Store charge when forward biased, must discharge when switching off. (2) Recovery time - 50-200ns for silicon fast diodes. (3) Recovery losses - Significant at high frequencies. (4) SiC SBD - Majority carrier device, no charge storage. (5) Zero recovery - Instantaneous switching. (6) Benefits - Higher efficiency, less EMI, faster switching. (7) Applications - Critical for PFC above 50kHz. SiC SBDs eliminate reverse recovery entirely, enabling highest efficiency designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC SBDs for any high-frequency application where efficiency matters.

faqs.question: How do I select the right SiC SBD for my application?
faqs.answer: SiC SBD selection criteria: (1) Voltage rating - 1.3x maximum DC bus voltage. (2) Current rating - 1.5x average forward current. (3) Package - TO-220, TO-247 based on thermal needs. (4) Forward drop - Lower is better for conduction losses. (5) Temperature - Check derating curves. (6) Parallel - Can parallel for higher current. (7) Cost - Balance performance vs budget. SCH2080KE is excellent for 650V/20A applications. Contact BeiLuo FAE for selection assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: SCH2080KE for 650V/20A; higher voltage/current options available.

faqs.question: What is the forward voltage drop of SCH2080KE?
faqs.answer: SCH2080KE forward characteristics: (1) Typical Vf - 1.35V at 20A, 25°C. (2) At 125°C - ~1.25V (negative tempco). (3) At 10A - ~1.25V. (4) Comparison - Similar to silicon ultrafast diodes. (5) Benefit - No reverse recovery losses offset Vf. (6) Total losses - Lower than silicon in most applications. (7) Thermal - Lower losses at high temperature. The forward drop is competitive and the zero recovery makes it superior overall.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider total losses (conduction + switching) when comparing to silicon.

faqs.question: Can SCH2080KE be used in parallel for higher current?
faqs.answer: SCH2080KE parallel operation: (1) Positive tempco - Good current sharing. (2) Thermal runaway - Not an issue with SiC SBDs. (3) Layout - Symmetrical connections essential. (4) Current sharing - Typically within 10% with good layout. (5) Derating - Use 90% of combined rating. (6) Applications - 40A+ rectifiers. (7) Benefits - Cost-effective for high current. Parallel operation is straightforward due to positive temperature coefficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can parallel 2-3 devices for 40-60A applications.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency improvement with SiC SBD vs silicon diode?
faqs.answer: Efficiency improvement with SiC SBD: (1) PFC applications - 0.5-1% efficiency gain. (2) Switching frequency - Can increase 2-3x. (3) Loss reduction - Eliminate reverse recovery losses. (4) EMI - Reduced due to soft switching. (5) Heatsink - 20-30% smaller possible. (6) System cost - Often lower total cost. (7) Payback - Immediate in most applications. SiC SBDs provide measurable efficiency and system benefits.
faqs.decisionGuide: SiC SBDs pay for themselves through efficiency gains and reduced cooling.

faqs.question: Is SCH2080KE suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: SCH2080KE automotive qualifications: (1) AEC-Q101 - Full automotive qualification. (2) Grade 0 - -40°C to +175°C. (3) PPAP available - For production approval. (4) Reliability - Proven in EV OBC applications. (5) Screening - 100% screening. (6) Traceability - Full lot traceability. (7) Quality - Japanese manufacturing standards. Fully qualified for automotive including EV onboard chargers and DC-DC converters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully qualified for automotive; ideal for EV OBC and DC-DC applications.

products.name: SiC Schottky Barrier Diodes SCH2080KE

products.shortDescription: 1200V 20A SiC Schottky Barrier Diode with zero reverse recovery, designed for high-voltage PFC and rectification in 800V EV systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The SCT2120AF is a 1200V 20A Silicon Carbide Schottky Barrier Diode featuring zero reverse recovery charge and fast switching characteristics. This device is specifically designed for high-voltage applications including 800V EV systems, high-voltage industrial power supplies, and solar inverters.

The SiC Schottky technology eliminates reverse recovery losses, enabling high-efficiency operation at switching frequencies up to 100kHz and beyond. The 1200V rating provides adequate margin for 800V battery systems and high-voltage DC bus applications.

With its high-voltage capability and excellent switching performance, the SCT2120AF is the ideal choice for next-generation 800V EV onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and high-voltage industrial power supplies.
features: Fast switching with no reverse recovery

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive
faeReview.content: The SCT2120AF is essential for 800V EV designs. The 1200V rating provides the necessary margin for 800V battery systems, and the 20A rating handles most OBC and DC-DC applications. Like all SiC SBDs, it has zero reverse recovery, which is critical for achieving 97%+ efficiency in OBC designs. I've used this extensively in 800V EV platforms with excellent results. Pair it with SCT3080KL or SCT3040KL MOSFETs for a complete high-efficiency solution. The automotive qualification gives confidence for production designs.
faeReview.highlight: Essential 1200V SiC SBD for 800V EV applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower voltage and cost for 400V applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 400V EV and standard industrial
Use for high-power OBC and industrial

faqs.question: Why is 1200V rating needed for 800V EV systems?
faqs.answer: 1200V rating necessity: (1) 800V battery - Nominal voltage, up to 850V max. (2) PFC output - ~900V DC bus. (3) Margin - 1.3x safety margin required. (4) Transients - Voltage spikes during switching. (5) Reliability - Higher voltage rating improves long-term reliability. (6) 650V devices - Insufficient margin for 800V. (7) Industry standard - 1200V is standard for 800V systems. The 1200V rating provides necessary safety margin and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use 1200V devices for 800V EV systems; 650V insufficient.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SCH2080KE and SCT2120AF?
faqs.answer: SCH2080KE vs SCT2120AF comparison: (1) Voltage - 650V vs 1200V. (2) Current - Both 20A. (3) Applications - 400V vs 800V systems. (4) Forward drop - 1.35V vs 1.45V. (5) Cost - SCT2120AF higher due to higher voltage. (6) Package - Both TO-220. (7) Selection - SCH2080KE for 400V, SCT2120AF for 800V. Both offer zero reverse recovery and excellent performance in their respective voltage classes.
faqs.decisionGuide: SCH2080KE for 400V systems; SCT2120AF for 800V systems.

faqs.question: Can SCT2120AF be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: SCT2120AF for solar inverters: (1) Voltage - 1200V ideal for 1000V DC bus systems. (2) Current - 20A suitable for residential inverters. (3) Efficiency - Zero recovery enables 99%+ efficiency. (4) Frequency - Supports high switching frequency. (5) Reliability - Proven in outdoor applications. (6) Parallel - Can parallel for higher power. (7) Cost - Premium pays for itself in energy harvest. Excellent for solar inverter boost stages and rectifiers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 1000V DC bus solar inverters; can parallel for higher power.

faqs.question: What is the temperature coefficient of SCT2120AF?
faqs.answer: SCT2120AF temperature characteristics: (1) Forward voltage - Negative tempco (~-1mV/°C). (2) Current sharing - Good for parallel operation. (3) Thermal runaway - Not a concern. (4) High temp operation - Vf decreases, helping balance current. (5) Junction temp - Up to 175°C rated. (6) Derating - Check datasheet curves. (7) Design - Positive tempco simplifies parallel design. The temperature characteristics are favorable for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Negative Vf tempco aids current sharing in parallel configurations.

faqs.question: What is the switching speed of SCT2120AF?
faqs.answer: SCT2120AF switching characteristics: (1) Reverse recovery - Zero (no recovery time). (2) Turn-off - Instantaneous (capacitive only). (3) Capacitive charge - Low Qc. (4) Switching losses - Minimal compared to silicon. (5) Frequency - Can operate at 100kHz+. (6) EMI - Reduced due to no recovery current spike. (7) Efficiency - Higher due to no recovery losses. The switching performance is exceptional due to zero reverse recovery.
faqs.decisionGuide: Zero reverse recovery enables highest switching frequencies with minimal losses.

faqs.question: Is SCT2120AF qualified for automotive production?
faqs.answer: SCT2120AF automotive qualification: (1) AEC-Q101 - Fully qualified. (2) Grade 0 - -40°C to +175°C. (3) PPAP - Level 3 available. (4) Field proven - In production EVs. (5) Reliability - Extensive testing data. (6) Traceability - Full lot traceability. (7) Quality - Rohm automotive standards. Fully qualified and proven for automotive production including EV OBC and DC-DC applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully qualified for automotive production; PPAP available.

products.name: SiC Schottky Barrier Diodes SCT2120AF

categories.name: IGBTs
categories.description: High-performance IGBTs for industrial and automotive power applications.
categories.longDescription: Rohm IGBTs offer high efficiency and reliability for industrial motor drives, inverters, and power supplies. With voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V, Rohm IGBTs serve a wide range of power applications. Contact BeiLuo for Selection guidance. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

products.shortDescription: 650V 60A high-speed IGBT with integrated fast recovery diode, optimized for industrial motor drives and inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The RGTH60TK65D is a 650V 60A high-speed IGBT featuring an integrated fast recovery diode. This device is designed for industrial motor drives, inverters, and power supply applications where high efficiency and reliable switching performance are required.

The IGBT combines low saturation voltage with fast switching characteristics, enabling efficient power conversion at switching frequencies up to 30kHz. The integrated diode simplifies design and reduces component count.

With its 650V rating and 60A current capability, the RGTH60TK65D is ideal for 3-phase motor drives, solar inverters, UPS systems, and industrial power supplies. The TO-3P package provides excellent thermal performance for demanding applications.
features: TO-3P package for excellent thermal performance

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial
faeReview.content: The RGTH60TK65D is a solid choice for industrial motor drives and inverters. The 650V/60A rating covers most 3-phase applications up to ~30kW. The integrated diode is a nice feature that simplifies design and BOM. Switching performance is good for IGBTs - not as fast as SiC but much more cost-effective. Saturation voltage is competitive at 1.7V. For applications where SiC is too expensive but silicon MOSFETs can't handle the power, this IGBT hits the sweet spot. I've used it successfully in numerous motor drive and solar inverter projects.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective IGBT for 30kW class motor drives and inverters

alternativeParts.reason: More current for higher power applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between IGBT and SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: IGBT vs SiC MOSFET comparison: (1) Cost - IGBT lower cost per amp. (2) Efficiency - SiC 2-3% higher. (3) Switching - SiC 10x faster. (4) Frequency - IGBT up to 30kHz, SiC 100kHz+. (5) Voltage - IGBT better >1000V, SiC better <1200V. (6) Applications - IGBT for cost-sensitive, SiC for premium efficiency. (7) RGTH60TK65D - Good middle ground with integrated diode. Choose based on efficiency requirements and budget.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IGBT for cost-sensitive applications; SiC for maximum efficiency.

faqs.question: What applications is RGTH60TK65D best suited for?
faqs.answer: RGTH60TK65D optimal applications: (1) Motor drives - 3-phase up to 30kW. (2) Solar inverters - Residential and small commercial. (3) UPS - Online UPS systems. (4) Welding - Inverter welders. (5) Induction heating - Industrial heating equipment. (6) Power supplies - High-power SMPS. (7) PFC - Boost PFC stages. The 650V/60A rating with integrated diode makes it versatile for many industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 30kW class motor drives and industrial inverters.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency limit of RGTH60TK65D?
faqs.answer: RGTH60TK65D switching frequency: (1) Recommended - Up to 20kHz for best efficiency. (2) Maximum - 30kHz with increased losses. (3) Motor drives - Typically 4-16kHz. (4) Solar inverters - 16-20kHz typical. (5) Losses - Increase with frequency. (6) Thermal - Consider at high frequency. (7) Comparison - Lower than SiC (100kHz+). For higher frequencies, consider SiC MOSFETs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use up to 20kHz; consider SiC for frequencies above 30kHz.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is required for RGTH60TK65D?
faqs.answer: RGTH60TK65D gate drive: (1) Turn-on - +15V recommended. (2) Turn-off - 0V or negative bias (-5 to -8V). (3) Threshold - 5-6V typical. (4) Gate charge - ~150nC. (5) Drive power - ~2W at 20kHz. (6) Isolation - Required for bridge configurations. (7) Protection - Desaturation detection recommended. Standard IGBT gate drivers work well with this device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V turn-on with optional negative bias for fast turn-off.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand time of RGTH60TK65D?
faqs.answer: RGTH60TK65D short-circuit capability: (1) Withstand time - 10μs typical. (2) Detection - Must detect within 5-8μs. (3) Protection - Gate driver with desaturation detection. (4) Shutdown - Soft shutdown recommended. (5) Causes - Load short, bridge shoot-through. (6) Prevention - Proper dead time, current limiting. (7) Testing - Validate in application. Short-circuit protection is essential for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gate driver with desaturation detection and <8μs response time.

faqs.question: How does the integrated diode benefit my design?
faqs.answer: Integrated diode benefits: (1) BOM reduction - One less component. (2) Layout - Simpler PCB design. (3) Matching - Diode optimized for IGBT. (5) Cost - Lower than discrete solution. (6) Reliability - Factory matched pair. (7) Performance - Fast recovery matched to IGBT. The integrated diode simplifies design and improves reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Integrated diode simplifies design; ensure ratings meet application needs.

products.name: IGBTs RGTH60TK65D

products.shortDescription: 650V 80A high-speed IGBT with integrated fast recovery diode for high-power industrial motor drives and inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The RGTH80TK65D is a 650V 80A high-speed IGBT featuring an integrated fast recovery diode. This higher-current version of the RGTH series is designed for high-power industrial motor drives, large solar inverters, and industrial power supply applications.

The IGBT delivers low saturation voltage and fast switching performance, enabling efficient power conversion for applications up to ~50kW. The integrated fast recovery diode is matched to the IGBT for optimal performance in bridge configurations.
With its 80A current rating and robust TO-3P package, the RGTH80TK65D is ideal for demanding industrial applications where high reliability and efficient operation are essential.
features: TO-3P package for excellent thermal performance

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial
faeReview.content: The RGTH80TK65D extends the RGTH series to 80A for higher power applications. This device is perfect for 40-50kW motor drives and commercial solar inverters. The saturation voltage is excellent at 1.65V, even lower than the 60A version due to larger die size. The integrated diode handles the full 80A with fast recovery. For high-power industrial applications where SiC is still too expensive, this IGBT delivers excellent value. I've used it in large VFD and solar projects with great success.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost for medium power applications

faqs.question: What power level can RGTH80TK65D support?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D power capability: (1) Motor drives - Up to 50kW continuous. (2) Solar inverters - 30-50kW range. (3) Current - 80A at 25°C, ~60A at 100°C. (4) Voltage - 650V for 400V AC systems. (5) Switching - Up to 20kHz. (6) Thermal - Requires proper heatsinking. (7) Parallel - Can parallel for higher power. Suitable for high-power industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 40-50kW applications with proper thermal management.

faqs.question: How does RGTH80TK65D compare to RGTH60TK65D?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D vs RGTH60TK65D: (1) Current - 80A vs 60A (+33%). (2) Vce(sat) - 1.65V vs 1.7V (slightly better). (3) Cost - Higher due to larger die. (4) Applications - 40-50kW vs 20-30kW. (5) Package - Same TO-3P. (6) Switching - Similar performance. (7) Selection - Choose based on current requirements. Both offer excellent performance in their respective power classes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select based on current requirements; both offer excellent value.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for RGTH80TK65D?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D thermal design: (1) Rth(j-c) - 0.6°C/W typical. (2) At 60A: Pcond = 100W, Psw = 40W (20kHz). (3) Total - ~140W at full load. (4) Heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 0.5°C/W with forced air. (5) Interface - Thermal grease required. (6) Target - Tj < 150°C for long life. (7) Forced air - Recommended for continuous operation. Proper thermal design essential for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling with Rth(s-a) < 0.5°C/W for full power operation.

faqs.question: Can RGTH80TK65D replace older generation IGBTs?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D as replacement: (1) Pin compatible - Standard TO-3P. (2) Lower Vce(sat) - 10-15% improvement. (3) Faster switching - Reduced losses. (4) Higher temp - 175°C vs 150°C. (5) Drop-in - Direct replacement in most designs. (6) Efficiency - 1-2% improvement typical. (7) Reliability - Improved ruggedness. Excellent upgrade path for older designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Direct drop-in replacement with improved performance and efficiency.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency in motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D motor drive efficiency: (1) Inverter stage - 96-97% at full load. (2) System - 94-95% including motor. (3) vs SiC - 2-3% lower than SiC solution. (4) vs older IGBT - 1-2% improvement. (5) Frequency - Optimal at 8-16kHz. (6) Load - Peak efficiency at 75-100% load. (7) Cost efficiency - Best value for performance. Excellent efficiency for cost-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: 96-97% inverter efficiency; best value for cost-sensitive motor drives.

faqs.question: Is RGTH80TK65D suitable for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: RGTH80TK65D for solar inverters: (1) Voltage - 650V for 400V AC output. (2) Current - 80A supports 30-50kW. (3) Efficiency - 96-97% achievable. (4) Frequency - 16-20kHz typical. (5) Reliability - Proven in outdoor applications. (6) Cost - Lower than SiC for cost-sensitive designs. (7) Availability - Standard product, good supply. Excellent for commercial solar inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 30-50kW commercial solar inverters; cost-effective alternative to SiC.
